reviews.review:
Sent two cars right off a cliff and got an achievement for it. 
9/11, really good stuff. Keeps you entertained for 30 minutes, maybe 31.

This is supposedly a physics simulator, but the physics work differently every time you run the "simulation". The game has some charm to it, but plays terribly in comparision to almost any other simulator out there.

2/10

Physics are terrible (expected considering it's a damn cell phone game ported to PC) and there isn't much to be redeemed otherwise, laughably upcharged from the phone variety (which is $1-2, yet this is $10 with hardly, if any, extra content).

A game made in Unity, with an UI for tablets and a 'Share to Facebook!!' button at the end of every stage.
I think that sums it up quite nicely.

I used to be bridge constructor but i took arrow in knee. Well, this game make u feel like u are the best bridge contructor in this universe! 

If only u success on contruct the bridge.

I bought this game a while back and well. Money not well spent. The game just feels like a simple touchscreen game that one would play on the toliet. The graphics are not good nor bad and the gamplay gets boring very fast. I picked this game up as when I was a kid in primary school we used to have a really basic bridge building game on our PCs which prob ran windows 95 or 98. The game was very basic but I spent ages just building bridges then sending cars over them. That game was years better than this and thats why I cant give this game a positive score.

A fun physics puzzle for a while

I wish there were more puzzles that let you use all 4 building materials

3.5/5
Not a bad game really, but it's very very generic and simple. There are probably better Bridge building games out there.

This game isn't bad, persay, it's just that it could be a lot better. If you're low on cash and want a game like this, go right ahead and purchase it. However, if you don't mind dropping $20, just get The Bridge Project.

Great insight into the world of engineering. While the physics involved may not be accurate at times, it offers a jocund take on imaginarian bridge construction. The green-red visual allows you to identify any weak points, which is an added plus. Personally, I would use that to try out some crazy ideas - Why not build a flying bridge?
Actually a fun game

The physics are broken, which really defeats the purpose of this game. There are much better architecural games for much cheaper. 

TL;DR: Broken physics, 0/100 would not play.

"If a man can bridge the gap between left and right, then maybe he was a great man."

This is a nice puzzle collection, if you like physics games. Word of warning: don't play for more than an hour at a time. You'll go crazy and become an engineer. Trust me on this.

Also, if you like this, look for an old Game Maker game called Bridging the Gap. That one is pretty good, too.

Timeless classic. But this one is not as good as those from (far) past. I remember myself playing Pontifex or how it was called. And even more than 10 years ago, that game was better than this one.

This one doesn't have the complexity of those old bridge builders. Only three materials here, you can't go 3D here (in Pontifex you could join triangles right between road lanes, here you go only 2D), tehre are no hydraulic bridges (those were fun in the past!), but you can select difficulty in this one. If you want achievements, you have to do those bridges pretty strong though. I like that.

Compared to the selection today, I will recommend this one, although I have played better bridge builders in the past.

And guess what - runs on Linux ;-)
Well, like other bridge constructing games, boring as always. The only pros of this games is its 3D graphics, compared to others.
GREATEST GAME EVER 11/10 -IGN

My three year old son is obsessed with this game, he plays it for 15 minutes every morning, helps him learn new words, and about balance/structure, which I suppose is good for his early year developments, and keeps him quiet for my morning cupper :)
Oddly addicting
Realistic Physics? Yeah right!

Was fun cracking the bridges, to the tank trucks. Had to make some wicked bridges anyways fun game, especially when everything explodes 10/10. 

Also added 3 guides for the truck / tank truck solutions for the last 3 regions. ^.^ ENJOY.

A truck exploded and went into space

10/10

This game doesn't revolutionize anything, it's the standard bridge builder. But it's a nice game! I've seen some other reviews talking about bugged physics but I just finished every level and nothing weird ever happened.

The definitely-made-for-mobile interface can be a turn off for some people, but it does its job very well, it's not a poor port. The fact that using the right mouse click is very useful goes to prove that, in my opinion.

Some levels have a 'crazy bridge' sign, meaning you'll need to build some outrageous bridge to beat that level. These are very fun, of course, but the construction method start to feel weak in these levels. Feels like you should have more control over the building, like defining exactly what would be solid and what wouldn't etc (these things are automatically defined depending on the angle and adjacent pieces).

While I do recommend this game, judging by the fact that it isn't anything new and it's fairly short, I'd say wait for a sale or something. At full price I'm not so sure if it's worth it.

Best game ever!
should be called truck jump constructor though

While it would be nice to have more details about how different kinds of bridges are built, rather than just, essentially, throwing the user into the pool and hoping they swim, this is a good game.  If there was more detailed feedback as to how the various stresses and strains operated on the various parts of the structure, it would make for a far more educational tool.
It satisfys my fetish for bridges, 10/10
It's fun, really. Playing with your best friend, and comparing your bridges makes it even better!
Pretty bridgey if you ask me...

There are objects in the world.  Some fall under the category 'Games'.  Some fall under the category 'Art'.  But is there a category for an object that falls under both?  There is.  That category is called 'Bridge Constructor'.

Perhaps you think, "No! Bridge Constructor is not an art game; it is an engineering game."  To which I say, "Non Monsieur/Madame/Mademoiselle, non!"  Then I finish my thought in English because that is all the French I know.  Remember, you are [b][i]not actually constructing bridges in the real physical world[/i][/b] (apologies if you thought that this game might let you do that).  You are creating simulations, models, dreams of your inner self.  What you create on screen is you; you are what you create on screen.  I repeat: once you play this game, there will be bridges inside of you.

It should also be known that this game tells the story of a world.  Do you wonder what drives the cars, the trucks, the tankers?  Wonder no more my friend: it is a race of little insect people.  Ants?  Bees?  Weevils?  I do not know: insects.  All I know is that they cannot fly.  If they could, they would not need bridges.

-------------- The Story of JD7QW --------------

Designate JD7QW buckled the seatbelt around his carapaced waist, its legs quivering as it did so.  It tried to hide its fright, but even from outside the tanker truck the Planner noticed.

"JD7QW," the Planner said, its mandibles clicking furiously, "this is no time for fear.  You are performing the function for which you were produced, as am I.  By testing this bridge we are expanding the reach of the Colony and celebrating the greatness of the Queen.  That is all we can do.  That is all we shall do."

Not daring to look at the Planner, JD7QW nodded while fixing its gaze straight ahead.  The bridge in front of it was very wooden and very straight.  It also appeared very fragile, like sunlight on water.  How could it hold the weight of the enormously flammable tanker?  "I imagine that there are supports underneath?" JD7QW asked, doing its best to hide its anxiety.  "Steel?  Perhaps concrete?"

"Such questions!" the Planner snapped.  "I will answer, but know that I will be filing a Protest when I return to the Colony."  It shook its engorged head.  "It appears that the Breeders are producing defective Designates."

Again JD7QW did not dare turn around.  Instead it looked up.  The sky was cloudless and blue, so clear that the ghost of the moon could be faintly seen even in the daytime.  [i]I would like to fly there[/i], the Designate thought suddenly.  [i]Such wonders I would see![/i]  But of course it couldn't.  Only the Queen was allowed to have wings.

The Planner continued.  "There is no steel.  There is no concrete.  There is wood and only wood.  But -"  The Planner's voice softened.  Surprised, JD7QW peeked over.  The Planner's mandibles had slowed, its multi-faceted eyes had grown clouded and dreamy.  "- but oh, the design, the joins and triangles and descending supports, the interlocking wheels of pressure and release, the. . ."  Caught up in the Planner's vision, it allowed itself to think: [i]Someday I shall be allowed to dream as well.[/i]

Then the Planner shook its head.  Its voice turned to jagged stone.  "No more!  You are impudent, JD7QW.  Drive now.  Go."

It stepped back.  Unwilling and unable to disobey, JD7QW depressed the gas pedal and the tanker truck rumbled forward.

A short segment of paved road lay between the truck and the bridge.  It took both an instant and an eternity to cross.  Then JD7QW felt the wheels beneath him [i]clunk[/i] as it rattled onto the wooden bridge, and right away it knew that it would not survive that day.

The bridge was already beginning to sway, the surface to buckle.  A series of grand snapping noises echoed within the ravine, each one like the shattering of an enormous tree.  The truck began to [i]descend[/i]; frantic, JD7QW pressed the gas pedal with all its strength.  The truck jerked forward, and for a moment the Designate thought that the vehicle might simply plow through the air to the other side, an invincible and impossible metal beast.

Then the wooden surface below him imploded, and the truck fell in earnest.

JD7QW barely had time to squeeze its eyes shut, to gag at the vortex in its stomach, when the fuel tank exploded.  Free of its burden, the cab of the truck shot up into the air, and the sudden reverse in sensation caused JD7QW's eyes to fly open in surprise.  And what it saw through the broken windshield was blue, endless blue, with the ghost of the moon traced far above.

It knew there wasn't much time left.  With legs that were no longer trembling, it pressed the release on the seat buckle.

The Designate shot out of the vehicle immediately.  Below him, the truck cab had already slowed and was falling back to earth.  [i]But not me[/i], JD7QW thought.  [i]Now I shall fly.  I shall fly to the moon.[/i]  And it began to smile as it dreamed of what it might find there.

----------------------------------------

There is a JD7QW inside all of us.  If not for yourself, play this game for it.
It won't get any points for realism, but most of the fun comes from the crazy physics.

Great game to help pass the time if your looking for something quick and simple.. Even though it is a quite simple game, it's far from easy. You just may have to use a little extra brain power to complete some of the levels in this game.. It's really fun and frustrating at the same time.. lol.. I think its a pretty good game overall.
The game is a lot of fun. 9/10

9/10

If you like bridge building games, This is the best one i have played yet.
Uses not only the normal wood but cables and pillars too (and some more i have not unlocked yet)

If you do not like bridge building games, don't buy it!

The physics are much better than all the others i have played (many for android and PC)
And you can test your bridge with cars, vans, or a oil truck. Lots of levels, with many nice backgrounds.

The title says it all, you construct bridges. There's a fairly amount of challenge to the puzzles mostly due to the tight budjets, but you can get very creative with the constructs because the physics model is a bit on the silly side.
This is a game for you if you like building puzzles and something like World of Goo no longer sate the apetite.
Great puzzle game, but the controls are a little clumsy.

Great game, got in a bundle. Didn't expect much, but it is way better than bridge project. Its almost as good as bridge it but lacking just a few features as far as I can tell. But its still great. Good that it has the finer grid mode so that you can make bridges more accurate just like in bridge it. Doesn't have hydraulics or interactive modes like in bridge it, but enough comparison, this game is great by itself. The world map going from bridge to bridge is nice. The physics are good enough for me. Basically Its nice to have a bridge game other than bridge it that does not suck (bridge project). I'm happy I have this game. Its simple but challenging. Its pretty easy to make a bridge that cars can cross, but when you get higher than that is when the challenge comes in. worth it. (at least in the bundle I got it in it was the only worthwhile game.)
A nice casual game you can sit down and play if you have 30 to spare. I'll be honest, all I do is send trucks to their death. But it sure is fun!

Picked this game up a few days ago with the most recent Humble Bundle and I'm getting a serious kick out of it. I was looking for a bridge building fix after trying the "Bridge It" demo but wasn't looking to spend much $ towards that end. In steps Humble Bundle to save the day, check it out.

This one is rather shallow compared to other games with the same premise. Particularly glaring limitations are no free rotation, no turning the stress-test colors off, and worst of all no sharing of custom levels or even saving your solutions externally. Avoid unless you are a very casual player or getting it as part of a bundle (like the current Humble); there are better choices to scratch this itch.
A rather calming and simple casual game. A good way to kill an hour or 2.
As an engineer, I find this to be a pretty fun bridge building game.
It is a fun time waster when you have some extra time. Nothing extrordinary, but fun none the less.
Built a seriously unstable bridge, my trucks managed to be flipped over the finish line as the bridge was plummetting to the ground and I still passed the mission..... 12/10 should buy.

Feels a lot like a mobile game, not sure if I like that...
The UI is clunky, placing objects feels loose, and the overall game is too easy.
Certainly feels as if this game is best suited for mobile touch controls.
Not worth $10.
Possibly the best game of the last decade.

It's not such a bad game. Only buy it when it's on sale, though.

The biggest issue I have with it is that what seems to work with smaller and lighter vehicles often does not work at all for the bigger and heavier vehicles, making the bridge collapse. Thinking up a whole new game plan for the same mission every time gets tedious quickly. But all in all, it's alright if you get it for cheap and want to waste a couple of hours putting pieces of wood and metal in certain places... ;)

This game is awesome if you are looking for a simple one hand puzzle game. If you other hand is full with... you know. you second monitor users know what I am talking about, this game would be great for you!
Fun enough to jump on for 10 - 15 minuets of casual fun. Getting trucks across your bridges can be difficult and annoying. It is a puzzle game after all, and would not say its a bridge simulator.

On the surface, this game appears to be shovelware. Half-finished collision boxes and clipart menu graphics leave a bad first impression, but play into it a bit, and at its core are a collection of solid and well thought-out physics puzzles.

My main problem with the game is that the physics are not consistent, which can be a severe detriment to a puzzle game (See Bad Rats), but here it doesn't seem to be a problem at all unless you're going for the "Your bridge broke but you still made it" achievement.

I was surprised by the creativity of the puzzles. The first bridge that gives you a "Checkpoint", or a location your vehicles must drive through before reaching the goal, places the checkpoint almost directly below the starting location, forcing you to think up some insane motocross ramp to reach it safely. And it only gets wackier from there.

Overall a thoroughly enjoyable puzzle game, if you like that sort of thing, with only a few problems that almost never seem to get in the way of gameplay.
WARNING: This game contains ads for Facebook. After every level you are greeted with a "Share this on Facebook" ad. Barf. Don't buy this game if your privacy is a concern. NO REFUNDS!
This game is quite awesome. I cant wait to get another copy on sale.

Garbage clone of Pontifex/BCS/Bridge-It. Horrible physics, simplistic levels, copycat clone with no innovation that does nothing well at all. The interface feels like a poorly made webpage. Did I mention the graphics are horribly, horribly ugly? Any veteran bridge builder who understands the first sentence in this review should IMMEDIATELY write this game off, as it doesn't even take 60 seconds after loading to realize you've wasted your money. I usually keep my reviews private to friends only, but I want to warn other people out there who might stupidly waste their money like I did. THIS GAME IS A COMPLETE AND TOTAL RIP OFF.

Bought this game on sale for 3.71$ US. Before I bought it I didn't know that it was a port from mobile device. On that platform it's only 0.99$ US so compared to PC 9.99$ it's a bit pricey. Yes, the UI does scream MOBILE GAME as some people have stated, but that doesn't stop it from being fun and challenging.

The simplicity or exact port from mobile to PC actually makes it really easy to navigate and focus on the game / puzzle at hand instead of searching for things you need to build.

I would recommend it on solid grounds of;
Simplicity
Challenging
Easy to get into ( the point of 99% of mobile gaming)
Fun.

I wouldn't recommend on grounds of price, 9 times more than mobile? Just buy it on sale or during a bundle.

In essence a puzzle game where the premise is building a bridge but you are restricted in the materials you can use as well as a budget.   Surprisingly entertaining and a great timer killer at a reasonable price.  If you're into that sort of thing anyway.

Challenging, relaxing puzzler with a fairly large level set. Good physics are complimented by simple, clean graphics. Definitely a good purchase at sale price. 9/10

Relaxing casual game, great for buying when on sale. Graphics look really good, and gameplay is just like the android app. It's just what is expected when playing it on a computer, but you won't be surprised.
Half the bridges can be completed without supports, often time supports dont work properly anyway. overall... meh?

Physics games are always fun, and this one is no exception. Watching the trucks drive carelessly over your "I-never-thought-it-would-hold" creations is engrossing!

I'm not sure it's worth the full price, but at 3€ it's a no brainer!
Great game, reminds me of the old West Point Bridge builder program i used in in my Engineering Classes
FUCK YES! i apply for engineering jobs now, this game changed my life
Found out if I was an engineer, we would all be dead.

I recommend this game but only because I have had so much fun with my friends as we scream and cry at trucks as they fall through our poorly built bridges. If I was an antisocial type who played this game alone, I would have hated it. 

The "campaign" is reasonably long, but it gets repetitive. The only way to add variety is to try stupid designs that would not work in real life, but do in game. For example, I used a counter-weight to create enought tension to support my bridge. The levels are very challenging, but not in the sense that they challenge your understanding of physics, because anyone who knows anything about building bridges with get very frustrated with the idiotic placement of build-points in the map. In combination with the restricted use of certain materials, and the third-world budget for purchasing said materials, bridges often can barely stand their own weight, and beating a level often only means that your load got a cross even though the bridge collapsed behind it.

Which brings me to the reason I actually recommend it. The level of challenge results in hilariously inadequate structures that make any educated person cringe. It's addictive to try something hilarious and then watch as cars plummet to their death with satisfying crunches. In a sense, the game is more fun to lose than to win. And if you have a group of friends nearby who can make fun of your attempts to build and when you fail push you aside to attempt their own idiotic designs, it gets fun and competitive. However, this is not a feature of the game, but a feature the way you can choose to augment your experience.

My chief complaint, though, is the lack of a sandbox mode. The game never gives you a chance to experiment with different properties of physics or the materials you are working with without restricting you to a budget and limited build points. And a sandbox mode would not be hard to implement, considering that all it would need is custom maps, build points, and unlimited access to the already available resources.

So, if you are looking for an educational experience that will teach you to build bridges . . . go somewhere else.

If you are looking for a hilarious cautionary showcase of how not to build bridges, particularly with smug friends nearby, buy Bridge Constructor. (Which should have been named "Bridge Destructor")
i want my money back...
this game sucks
Great game. Get it if you like building or like making cars fly of a bridge.

The epic story of a civil rights campaigner, whose legacy led so many to cross the divides throughout society, that led to his affectionate nickname the "Bridge Constructor". In this multi-generational adventure, follow the titular character from his humble beginnings as a penny-buffer, through his meteoric rise to champion of the people. As the Bridge Constructor always said, "I have a beam.."

Its a bridge constructor game. Sure, its probably one of the best if not the best but it really isn't worth the price if you ask me. It has almost no replay value. If you wanna blow your money and like games like these, then go ahead. Otherwise, just forget about this game.

I'm not recommending this game only because it is a little pricy for what you are getting.  It does have a fantastic physics engine and the graphics (although simple) are polished.  I expected it to be a little more sandbox and have a few other features and building materials.  If I had to rate the game, it would be a 5/10.

Yes it's just a copy of advanced bridge construction games.
Yes it's a mobile port....
But still, it's a well done little game where you can enjoy yourself for some time.
Fairly light puzzle game with a fantastic theme. Some of the levels are disappointingly only solved by improbably ugly and unstable constructions, but the majority can result in elegant designs.

This is a great game that reminds me of the old Bridge Builder game they had in Elementry School. Use wood, steel, etc to build a bridge accross a span. The only thing I don't like is you are limited to a budget and you can't build just anything. But over all it is a fun game, with many challenges.

I don't know why this game was greenlit, it's a shame to all the other bridge building games. The UI screams "I AM THE MOBILE PHONE GAME" and the physics are absolutely terrible:  http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=odGkRXc5R4k 

If you are deciding to buy this game, check out the other bridge building game (Bridge It) that is here on Steam, it is way better.
really fun design game that is pretty good. should def try it out
